title: Karachi Style Fish Fry

seo.title: Karachi Fish Fry Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Seafood Dish
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Karachi Fish Fry at home! This authentic Pakistani recipe features crispy, flavorful fish, perfect for any meal. Get the step-by-step guide now!
longTailKeywords: how to make Karachi Fish Fry
Karachi Fish Fry step by step
best Karachi Fish Fry at home
h2Headings: What Makes Karachi Fish Fry Special?
Ingredients for Authentic Karachi Fish Fry
How to Make Karachi Fish Fry: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Crispiest Karachi Fish Fry
Serving Your Delicious Karachi Fish Fry
Variations & Substitutions for Karachi Fish Fry
seo.imageAlt: A plate of golden-brown Karachi Fish Fry, garnished with lemon wedges and fresh cilantro, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Crispy, flavorful, and perfectly spiced – our authentic Karachi Fish Fry brings the taste of Pakistan right to your kitchen!

description: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani street food, renowned for its incredibly crispy exterior and tender, flavorful fish. Marinated in a unique blend of spices and herbs, this dish offers a delightful culinary experience that captures the vibrant essence of Karachi's coastal cuisine. It is a perfect balance of tang, spice, and crunch, making it a must-try for seafood lovers.

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Wash the fish fillets thoroughly and pat them dry with paper towels. Make diagonal scores on both sides of the fish to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. Set aside.

instructions.title: Mix the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, crushed carom seeds, and salt. Mix well to form a thick paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Fish
instructions.text: Generously apply the spice paste to both sides of each fish fillet, ensuring it gets into the scores. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 1-2 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Coating Batter
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine gram flour and rice flour. Gradually add 2-3 tablespoons of water to create a thick, lump-free batter. It should be thick enough to cling to the fish, not runny. You may add a pinch of salt to the batter if desired.

instructions.title: Coat and Heat Oil
instructions.text: Dip each marinated fish fillet into the gram flour batter, ensuring it is evenly coated on all sides. In a deep frying pan or wok, heat cooking oil over medium-high heat until it reaches about 175°C (350°F).

instructions.title: Fry the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place 2-3 coated fish fillets into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry for 4-6 minutes on each side, or until the fish is golden brown and crispy. The internal temperature should reach 63°C (145°F). Remove the fried fish with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack or paper towels to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Repeat the frying process with the remaining fish fillets. Serve the hot and crispy Karachi Style Fish Fry immediately with a squeeze of fresh lemon, mint chutney, or tamarind sauce. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

tips: For extra crispiness, ensure your oil is at the correct temperature before frying. If the oil is not hot enough, the fish will absorb too much oil and become greasy.
Do not overcrowd the frying pan; fry the fish in batches to maintain oil temperature and ensure even cooking and crispiness.
Serve immediately for the best taste and texture. Reheating can make the fish less crispy.
article: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani Seafood dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
